title: D2 ProblemDetails spec
description: Schema for contracts/problem-details/problem-details.spec.json — the source-of-truth catalog that DcsvIo.D2.ProblemDetails.SourceGen reads to emit the D2ProblemDetailsKeys static class into DcsvIo.D2.ProblemDetails.Abstractions (consumed by DcsvIo.D2.Auth.Http and DcsvIo.D2.AspNetCore), and the TypeScript codegen pipeline reads to emit problem-details.g.ts into @dcsv-io/d2-problem-details-abstractions (host apps may re-export the same constants).

typeUriPrefix.description: Base URI for the RFC 7807 ProblemDetails 'type' field. The runtime appends the kebab-cased error code to this prefix (e.g. 'https://problems.d2.dcsv.io/' + 'auth-bearer-missing'). Must end with a trailing slash.

contentType.description: MIME type emitted on responses carrying an RFC 7807 ProblemDetails body. Per RFC 7807 §6.1, the IANA-registered value is 'application/problem+json' for JSON bodies. Single source of truth so .NET middleware (JwtAuthMiddleware) and TS BFF guards write byte-identical Content-Type headers.

extensionKeys.description: Closed catalog of extension keys emitted on the RFC 7807 ProblemDetails body. Includes both D2-private d2_-namespaced keys (d2_error_code, d2_messages, d2_input_errors) and unprefixed keys aligned with external conventions (traceId per W3C-OTel; correlationId per HTTP X-Correlation-Id header).

titles.description: Closed catalog of human-readable 'title' values keyed by HTTP status. Exactly one entry MAY carry httpStatus=null as the fallback title used when no httpStatus row matches.

constName.description: Public C# / TS constant identifier — UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. Must be unique across the spec.

value.description: Wire-format extension key emitted on the RFC 7807 ProblemDetails JSON body (e.g. 'd2_error_code', 'traceId'). Must be unique across the spec.

doc.description: XML <summary> / JSDoc text rendered on the emitted constant.

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.

constName.description: Public C# / TS constant identifier — UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. Must be unique across the spec.

httpStatus.description: HTTP status this title maps to (e.g. 401, 503). null marks the fallback title used when no httpStatus row matches. At most one entry MAY carry httpStatus=null.

value.description: Wire-format title value emitted on the RFC 7807 ProblemDetails JSON body (e.g. 'Unauthorized', 'Service Unavailable'). Locale-NEUTRAL English from a closed enumeration — locale-aware translation is the client's job via the d2_messages extension. Must be unique across the spec.

doc.description: XML <summary> / JSDoc text rendered on the emitted constant.

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.
